channel.title: KiddieDaily — News for Families
channel.description: Daily kid-friendly news with bias indicators and fact checks. Updated every morning.

item.title: Watch: Fans react to tense World Cup game
item.description: England Captain Harry Kane rescued England from a major upset, with two late goals, after DR Congo took an early lead. — 5 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Quiz of the Week: Have you been following this week's news?
item.description: It's been a busy week of big stories and headlines. Have you been keeping up with the news over the last seven days? Test your knowledge of the latest top stories with our Quiz of the Week. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Lone star ticks are covering much of the U.S. Here's what you need to know
item.description: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome. — 4 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Hymn to Nungal: A Praise Song for the Sumerian Goddess of Prisons
item.description: The Hymn to Nungal (circa 1894-1595 BCE) is a Sumerian poem praising Nungal, the goddess of prisons and rehabilitation (also associated with the underworld), as well as the prison house she presided o — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: The blueprint for building a fairer world without breaking the planet
item.description: A group of more than 40 researchers spent 20 months devising a plan for the world to achieve ecological sustainability within planetary boundaries, all while seeing incomes rise for 98% of the global — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Good News in History, July 1
item.description: 123 years ago today, the starting pistol rang out for the first Tour de France, the most prestigious cycling competition today. Set up and sponsored by the newspaper L’Auto, ancestor of the curr — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: High Tech Jacket Prototype Pulls Drinking Water From Thin Air – Up to 1.5 Pints Per Day
item.description: A new high tech jacket developed by engineers at the University of Texas can pull drinking water from thin air. With the advance in fabric technology, the jacket can collect up to one-and-a-half pints — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Antarctica's first dinosaur fossil belonged to a group of the largest land animals ever
item.description: Researchers have identified the first-ever dinosaur fossil discovered on Antarctica, revealing it belonged to a titanosaur. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Sea level rise is ruining coastal Bangladesh with salty water (commentary)
item.description: Every morning, before the sun has fully risen over the tidal flats of Satkhira in southwest Bangladesh, women begin walking. They walk two kilometers, sometimes five (about 1.2 to 3.1 miles) and somet — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Largest and Most Detailed Picture Taken of Milky Way’s Center–With Millions of Stars and Thousands of Worlds
item.description: It took a new and special space telescope to parse out each one of these individual stars gleaming in a dizzying mass at the center of our own dear Milky Way galaxy. The European Space Agency’s — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: A whopping 14 million species of insects — or more — may roam Earth
item.description: New calculations suggest that the insect species inhabiting our planet may be double or triple previous estimates. — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Why does metal stick together in space?
item.description: If you push two metal plates together on Earth, nothing happens. In space, they can fuse into one. Here's why. — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Scientists Say: Fluoride
item.description: This electrically charged form of fluorine boasts powerful tooth-strengthening abilities. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Scientists Find a Razor-Sharp Line of Earthquakes Hidden Beneath Alaska
item.description: Huh?? ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Tennis superstar makes a big entrance at Wimbledon
item.description: The Grand Slam winner showed off her latest walk-on outfit for the Wimbledon crowds. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.
